_meta.description: Company universe — one record per investable name across all 13 verticals. Fields: ticker, name, vertical, chain_layer, geo, structure, thesis_note, conviction. Conviction is a first-pass placeholder — re-rank as you add memos/notes. Enrich over time.

companies.name: Synopsys
companies.thesis_note: Half of the EDA duopoly; broadest design+IP flow. Gates all advanced design. Export-control on/off lever to China.

companies.name: Cadence Design Systems
companies.thesis_note: Other half of EDA duopoly; emulation (Palladium) strength. High-margin recurring; AI design tailwind.

companies.name: Arm Holdings
companies.thesis_note: Dominant CPU IP; royalty model into every phone + growing datacenter. Arm China JV is a control wrinkle.

companies.name: Alphawave Semi
companies.thesis_note: High-speed SerDes + HBM PHY + UCIe IP — the connectivity layer of AI chip design. M&A target interest.

companies.name: Empyrean Technology
companies.thesis_note: China's leading EDA firm; cannot match full-flow leading-edge but the national champion under sanctions pressure.

companies.name: ASML Holding
companies.thesis_note: Sole EUV maker — the single deepest chokepoint in tech. High-NA ramping at Intel/Samsung. The toll booth of leading-edge.

companies.name: Applied Materials
companies.thesis_note: Broadest WFE portfolio (~18% share); also leads hybrid bonding into advanced packaging. Service base recurring.

companies.name: Lam Research
companies.thesis_note: Etch + deposition leader; critical for 3D NAND and gate-all-around. China exposure a swing factor.

companies.name: KLA Corporation
companies.thesis_note: Near-monopoly in process control/inspection — yield is the gating economic variable on every new node.

companies.name: Tokyo Electron
companies.thesis_note: Japan's WFE champion; coat/develop (with ASML litho) + etch/deposition. Core to every fab line.

companies.name: VAT Group
companies.thesis_note: Near-monopoly in vacuum valves inside every deposition/etch tool — a hidden single-source subsystem chokepoint.

companies.name: Advantest
companies.thesis_note: ATE duopoly with Teradyne; HBM/AI complexity lengthens test time = direct AI test-demand leverage.

companies.name: MKS Instruments
companies.thesis_note: Process subsystems (gas, vacuum, RF, lasers) + advanced packaging chemistry (Atotech). Broad WFE+packaging exposure.

companies.name: NAURA Technology
companies.thesis_note: China's largest WFE maker (~#8 globally); etch/deposition/clean. The localization bellwether ex-lithography.

companies.name: TSMC
companies.thesis_note: ~90%+ of <5nm + the CoWoS packaging chokepoint + COUPE photonics. The keystone of the entire AI hardware universe.

companies.name: Samsung Electronics
companies.thesis_note: Only IDM spanning DRAM/HBM/NAND + foundry + OLEDoS. Trails TSMC on leading-edge yield; HBM4 qual recovery key.

companies.name: Intel
companies.thesis_note: High-NA EUV lead customer (14A). US foundry turnaround optionality; execution + external-customer wins are the swing.

companies.name: UMC
companies.thesis_note: Mature-node foundry; display drivers, RF, power. Exposed to China mature-node overcapacity but stable cash flows.

companies.name: GlobalFoundries
companies.thesis_note: US/EU specialty foundry — automotive, RF, power. Western-supply-security premium; not leading-edge.

companies.name: SMIC
companies.thesis_note: China's foundry champion; 7nm via DUV multipatterning powers Huawei Ascend. Capped at ~7nm by export controls.

companies.name: Shin-Etsu Chemical
companies.thesis_note: Largest 300mm wafer maker (with SUMCO ~50%+); also photoresist. Quiet upstream Japan chokepoint under TSMC itself.

companies.name: SUMCO
companies.thesis_note: Second 300mm wafer giant. AI capacity ramp tightens wafer supply; pure-play wafer cycle exposure.

companies.name: SK Hynix
companies.thesis_note: HBM leader (~62%), Nvidia's lead supplier; MR-MUF process edge. The single best pure-play on the HBM bottleneck.

companies.name: Micron Technology
companies.thesis_note: Only US memory maker; HBM3E ramp overtook Samsung (~21%). US-supply-security + HBM growth dual thesis.

companies.name: Kioxia
companies.thesis_note: Japan NAND leader (ex-Toshiba). Note: verify listing/ticker — recently re-listed. NAND cycle + AI storage tier.

companies.name: SanDisk
companies.thesis_note: WD NAND spinoff; enterprise SSD + AI storage. Commoditized but cyclical-leverage play.

companies.name: Phison Electronics
companies.thesis_note: Controller leader; AI inference local-storage + memory-expansion angle. Taiwan-listed controller pure-play.

companies.name: ASE Technology
companies.thesis_note: World's largest OSAT (~45% of top-10); CoWoS overflow + advanced packaging ramp. Direct AI packaging leverage.

companies.name: Amkor Technology
companies.thesis_note: #2 OSAT; $7B Arizona advanced-packaging fab with Apple/TSMC — the US back-end reshoring play.

companies.name: BE Semiconductor
companies.thesis_note: Hybrid-bonding equipment leader — enables sub-10µm 3D stacking. High-margin chokepoint of advanced packaging.

companies.name: ASMPT
companies.thesis_note: Bonders + advanced packaging tools (TCB, hybrid bonding). Leveraged to the packaging capex cycle.

companies.name: Ibiden
companies.thesis_note: Top ABF substrate maker; high-layer-count substrates for big AI dies are supply-constrained. Japan substrate chokepoint.

companies.name: Unimicron
companies.thesis_note: ABF substrate leader (~22%). AI-die substrate demand + capacity tightness. Taiwan substrate pure-play.

companies.name: Ajinomoto
companies.thesis_note: Makes the ABF insulating film itself — a hidden near-monopoly upstream of every IC substrate. Food-company optionality.

companies.name: Kulicke & Soffa
companies.thesis_note: Bonding equipment; advanced packaging + thermocompression transition. Cyclical assembly-capex exposure.

companies.name: Nvidia
companies.thesis_note: ~80-90% merchant AI silicon; CUDA moat; Blackwell→Rubin. Also NVLink, BlueField DPU, Jetson Thor, $4B optics. The hub.

companies.name: Advanced Micro Devices
companies.thesis_note: MI300→MI400 (HBM4, 2nm) + Helios rack; the credible #2 merchant accelerator. ROCm software is the gating risk.

companies.name: Broadcom
companies.thesis_note: Designs Google TPU, Meta MTIA, OpenAI ASIC + dominant switch silicon + CPO. The diversified AI-infra compounder.

companies.name: Marvell Technology
companies.thesis_note: AWS Trainium + Microsoft Maia ASICs + optical DSP. Nvidia took a stake around NVLink Fusion. Custom-silicon #2.

companies.name: Alchip Technologies
companies.thesis_note: Turnkey backend ASIC design at advanced nodes (AWS et al.). Taiwan picks-and-shovels on the custom-ASIC wave.

companies.name: Global Unichip (GUC)
companies.thesis_note: TSMC-affiliated ASIC design service; advanced-node turnkey. Leverage to custom AI silicon proliferation.

companies.name: Cambricon
companies.thesis_note: China AI-chip champion; benefits from domestic-compute mandate + Nvidia export curbs. Capped by SMIC/HBM access.

companies.name: Arista Networks
companies.thesis_note: Ethernet leader for AI back-end networks; Ultra Ethernet beneficiary vs InfiniBand. Hyperscaler-concentrated.

companies.name: Astera Labs
companies.thesis_note: PCIe/CXL retimers + fabric switches + AECs — the AI-connectivity pure-play; UALink optionality. Premium valuation.

companies.name: Credo Technology
companies.thesis_note: ZeroFlap AECs dominant top-of-rack; signal-integrity at 224G. High-growth connectivity niche.

companies.name: Celestica
companies.thesis_note: Hyperscaler white-box networking + AI server ODM. Cheaper torque on the cluster-fabric buildout.

companies.name: Accton Technology
companies.thesis_note: Leading white-box switch ODM for hyperscalers. Taiwan networking-hardware pure-play.

companies.name: Hon Hai (Foxconn)
companies.thesis_note: Largest AI-server/rack assembler; GB200 NVL72 builds. Scale leader; thin margins but huge AI revenue ramp.

companies.name: Quanta Computer
companies.thesis_note: Major AI-server ODM; rev +66% YoY. Direct beneficiary of hyperscaler GPU-server demand.

companies.name: Wiwynn
companies.thesis_note: ~50% of hyperscale AI-server procurement; rev +149% YoY. The purest Taiwan AI-server ODM play.

companies.name: Super Micro Computer
companies.thesis_note: US AI-server leader; liquid-cooled rack-scale. Governance/accounting overhang is the key risk to weigh.

companies.name: Vertiv Holdings
companies.thesis_note: Datacenter power + thermal leader; liquid-cooling inflection (GB200 mandates it). Picks-and-shovels on power/cooling wall.

companies.name: Delta Electronics
companies.thesis_note: World-leading DC power supplies + power management; 48V-to-chip transition. Quiet AI-power compounder.

companies.name: Monolithic Power
companies.thesis_note: Power-management silicon for GPU boards; content-per-GPU rising with 1kW+ accelerators. High-quality compounder.

companies.name: Asia Vital Components
companies.thesis_note: Cold plates + thermal modules for AI servers; liquid-cooling ramp. Taiwan cooling pure-play.

companies.name: GE Vernova
companies.thesis_note: Gas turbines + grid for behind-the-meter AI power. The cross-link from datacenters into the energy supply chain.

companies.name: Coherent
companies.thesis_note: Vertically integrated lasers (EML) + transceivers + SiC optics. Nvidia ~$2B supply commitment. Optical chokepoint owner.

companies.name: Lumentum
companies.thesis_note: EML/DFB lasers + datacom optics; Nvidia ~$2B commitment locks supply. Leverage to 1.6T + laser scarcity.

companies.name: Fabrinet
companies.thesis_note: Builds transceivers for Nvidia/Cisco/Coherent (first $1B quarter). Thailand-based, geopolitically diversified picks-and-shovels.

companies.name: InnoLight Technology
companies.thesis_note: World #1 transceiver maker; with Eoptolink ~60% of Nvidia 800G. China assembly dominance — a quiet supply exposure.

companies.name: Eoptolink
companies.thesis_note: Fast-rising 800G/1.6T transceiver maker; gaining Nvidia share. China optical-module growth play.

companies.name: Mitsubishi Electric
companies.thesis_note: One of <5 EML laser makers — the deepest optical chokepoint. Diversified conglomerate; small but strategic laser unit.

companies.name: Harmonic Drive Systems
companies.thesis_note: Historic near-monopoly in harmonic reducers for robot joints — designed into Western humanoids. China localization is the risk.

companies.name: Nabtesco
companies.thesis_note: ~60% of precision RV reducers for mid-large industrial joints. The high-torque reduction-gear chokepoint.

companies.name: THK
companies.thesis_note: Linear motion (ball screws, rails); planetary roller screws are a humanoid bottleneck. Motion-component leader.

companies.name: NSK
companies.thesis_note: Bearings + ball/roller screws; humanoid linear-actuation content. Auto-cycle exposed but robotics optionality.

companies.name: Nidec
companies.thesis_note: World's largest motor maker; frameless/BLDC for robotics + EV. Broad motion exposure; China competition rising.

companies.name: Inovance
companies.thesis_note: China's motion-control champion vertically integrating motors/drives/screws into humanoids. The China localization core.

companies.name: Sanhua Intelligent
companies.thesis_note: Exclusive Tier-1 Tesla Optimus actuator assembler ($685M order). The clearest listed Optimus supply-chain proxy.

companies.name: Suzhou Green Harmonic
companies.thesis_note: China harmonic-reducer leader (~25%+ share); targeting Optimus supply via 500K-unit/yr plant. Localization beneficiary.

companies.name: Fanuc
companies.thesis_note: Industrial robotics 'Big Four'; CNC + arms. Incumbent base; humanoid wave is optionality, China share-loss is risk.

companies.name: Yaskawa Electric
companies.thesis_note: Servo drives + industrial arms; motion-control breadth. Automation + humanoid component optionality.

companies.name: Regal Rexnord
companies.thesis_note: Owns Kollmorgen/Allied Motion frameless torque motors used in humanoids. US motion-component exposure.

companies.name: Sony Group
companies.thesis_note: Dominant CMOS image sensors + OLEDoS micro-displays (Apple Vision Pro) + lasers. Multi-vertical sensing/display chokepoint.

companies.name: Hesai Group
companies.thesis_note: China lidar cost leader (>1.6M units 2025); Nvidia robotics partner. Fast-commoditizing but volume leader.

companies.name: Keyence
companies.thesis_note: Machine-vision + factory sensor powerhouse, ~50%+ margins. Automation breadth; premium quality compounder.

companies.name: Cognex
companies.thesis_note: Machine-vision pure-play; logistics + factory automation. Cyclical but robotics/vision secular tailwind.

companies.name: TDK
companies.thesis_note: InvenSense IMUs (ICM-42688 widely used in humanoids) + passives + batteries. Broad sensing/components exposure.

companies.name: Novanta
companies.thesis_note: Owns ATI 6-axis force/torque sensors — key to dexterous manipulation. Precision-sensing niche exposure.

companies.name: Intuitive Surgical
companies.thesis_note: Surgical robotics leader; razor-razorblade installed base. Adjacent end-market for precision robotics.

companies.name: Himax Technologies
companies.thesis_note: LCoS micro-displays + display driver ICs; AR design-ins. Taiwan-listed pure-play on AR display + driver IC.

companies.name: BOE Technology
companies.thesis_note: China display giant scaling OLEDoS + microLED for MR/AR. Volume + cost; China display ascendancy play.

companies.name: AU Optronics
companies.thesis_note: Panel maker pivoting to microLED + automotive/AR. Taiwan display transition exposure.

companies.name: ams OSRAM
companies.thesis_note: microLED + emitters + optical sensing. AR/automotive photonics; restructuring story with microLED optionality.

companies.name: MicroVision
companies.thesis_note: Laser beam scanning + lidar. Speculative AR/automotive display-tech option; high risk.

companies.name: Corning
companies.thesis_note: High-index glass for AR waveguides + optical fiber for AI clusters. Materials chokepoint bordering the hard-assets map.

companies.name: Vuzix
companies.thesis_note: Waveguide manufacturer + enterprise AR OEM; acquired ex-Google waveguide lab. Speculative AR pure-play.

companies.name: Qualcomm
companies.thesis_note: De-facto AR/XR SoC (Snapdragon AR) — the smart-glasses compute chokepoint. Diversified; AR is the call-option.

companies.name: Meta Platforms
companies.thesis_note: Ray-Ban Display breakout + Orion; the AI-glasses category creator + biggest AR R&D spender. Drives the whole AR BOM.

companies.name: Goertek
companies.thesis_note: Built the Ray-Ban Display light engine; dominant AR/VR device assembler. China assembly chokepoint for smart glasses.

companies.name: Luxshare Precision
companies.thesis_note: Major Apple/AR device assembler + components. China precision-assembly leverage to AR/wearables ramp.

companies.name: SICC (Tianyue)
companies.thesis_note: SiC substrates; emerging SiC AR waveguides (Meta Orion). Verify ticker. Speculative materials-chokepoint option.
